data.title: Microsoft's (MSFT) AI Chief Flags Superintelligence Risk, Sets Release Bar
data.description:
Mustafa Suleyman, head of Microsoft's (MSFT) artificial intelligence unit, has noted that superintelligence — intelligence that exceeds human knowledge — "comes with a great deal of risk" at this point in time.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Suleyman stated this in an interview with Bloomberg, noting that the U.S. tech giant would not release a superintelligence tool until it was assured that such a tool could be controlled. He emphasized that containment and alignment are necessary prerequisites.

Suleyman's comment comes about a month after Microsoft joined the race to develop superintelligence despite the global pushback against such technology. In late October, hundreds of prominent business leaders from around the world, including Apple (AAPL) co-founder Steve Wozniak, called for a ban on the development of superintelligence, warning of "potential human extinction" and other risks.

Microsoft Harps on 'Humanist' Approach to Superintelligence

Last month, Microsoft revealed that it has formed a team to develop what it called a "humanist" superintelligence. Suleyman, who became the leader of Microsoft's AI unit in March last year, defended this approach as targeted at solving real, concrete problems through technology that remains "grounded and controllable."

In his latest interview, the AI chief, who founded DeepMind, the AI research lab and startup acquired by Google (GOOGL) in 2014, further emphasized this approach. He urged other stakeholders in the tech industry to also adopt such a worldview.

Autonomous AI Still Experimental, Suleyman Says

Meanwhile, Suleyman also spoke about AI agents, noting that efforts to get AI to act autonomously are still at an experimental stage, which makes the technology currently prone to error. However, Microsoft has continued to ramp up its AI efforts.

Around mid-November, the American tech major unwrapped new tools added to its AI-powered assistant Copilot on the Microsoft 365 work productivity suite. The move followed the earlier launch of AI buddy 'Mico' for the more commonplace version of the AI assistant.

Is Microsoft a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

On Wall Street, analysts continue to hold a Strong Buy consensus rating on Microsoft's shares based on 32 Buys and two Holds issued over the past three months.

Moreover, the average MSFT price target of $632.22 implies approximately 31% upside from the current trading level.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Google Seeking British Brains to Boost Willow Quantum Chip Development
data.description:
Tech titan Google (GOOGL) is calling on British scientists to find real world solutions for its Willow quantum chip.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Get Access to Willow

Google Quantum AI has teamed up with the National Quantum Computing Centre in the U.K. to invite proposals from researchers to get access to the state-of-the-art Willow processor.

The new partners said that the collaboration seeks to advance the discovery of novel applications for quantum computers and accelerate progress toward quantum advantage over the most powerful classical computers.

UK researchers will be able to submit proposals describing their intended experiments or applications, the scientific rationale behind them, and how access to Willow could enable transformative results.

The successful research team will then work closely with experts from Google to design and conduct their experiments, while the NQCC will make available its technical expertise in support of the project. The NQCC will also offer research grants to fund researcher time for the successful UK research group.

The Quantum Adds Up

Several companies, including Google competitors, such as Microsoft (MSFT) and Amazon (AMZN), are pursuing their own hardware strategies, and the UK has emerged as a significant player. Quantinuum, which is headquartered in Cambridge and Colorado, reached a valuation of $10 billion in 2025, underscoring investor confidence in the sector.

Some tech experts believe that quantum machines may eventually solve problems in chemistry, medicine and materials that are beyond the reach of today's computers.

"The new ability to access Google's Willow processor, through open competition, puts UK researchers in an enviable position," Professor Paul Stevenson, of the University of Surrey, told the BBC. "It is good news for Google, too, who will benefit from the skills of UK academics."

It should also help boost Google's European growth – see below:
data.author: David Craik

data.title: 'It's a Real Turning Point,' Says Top Cowen Analyst About Boeing Stock
data.description:
Aerospace stock Boeing (BA) is finally showing signs of real improvement, according to top TD Cowen analyst Gautam Khanna who said the company has reached a "real turning point" as it heads into 2026. In his new note, Khanna kept his Buy rating on the stock with a $240 price target, indicating 21% upside potential.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Khanna said investor interest in aerospace is high, with stocks like Howmet (HWM), GE Aerospace (GE), and HEICO (HEI) trading at rich valuations. But Boeing has been left out of the rally due to past production problems. If execution keeps improving, he thinks the stock could catch up in 2026.

Why Cowen Is Upbeat About Boeing Stock Now

The 5-star analyst noted that production on the 737 and 787 programs has "turned the corner." With output improving, Boeing now has a clearer path to stronger cash flow. His model calls for about $2.8 billion in free cash flow in 2026, rising to $7.7 billion in 2027 and $11.7 billion in 2028, which implies a 6% free cash flow yield.

Khanna views that level as attractive for a company of Boeing's size. He also pointed to the recent integration of Spirit AeroSystems, which gives Boeing more control over its supply chain and should support smoother production over time.

Looking ahead, Khanna thinks demand for repair and service work in the industry will eventually level off, and that shift could push investors toward companies that build aircraft. If Boeing continues to execute, he sees it as the most likely stock to benefit.

It is worth noting that Khanna ranks 34 out of more than 10,000 analysts tracked by TipRanks. He has a success rate of 73%, with an average return per rating of 18.60% over a one-year timeframe.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 12/12/25 – U.S. Stock Futures Mixed as Investors Move Out of AI Plays
data.description:
U.S. stock futures traded mixed early Friday, after investors shifted from artificial intelligence (AI) stocks to other categories in the broader market following Oracle's (ORCL) fiscal second-quarter results. The database software and cloud computing company missed revenue expectations and sparked concerns about elevated AI spending. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) were down 0.21%, while futures tied to the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.03% and 0.29%, respectively, at 4:14 a.m. EST on December 12.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

During Thursday's regular trading session, the Dow Jones and the S&P 500 rose 1.34% and 0.21%, respectively, reaching record highs. Meanwhile, the Nasdaq Composite fell 0.26% as investors moved away from high-flying technology stocks.

Meanwhile, shares of chip company Broadcom (AVGO) were down 4.1% in Friday's pre-market trading despite reporting better-than-expected earnings for the fourth quarter of Fiscal 2025. Meanwhile, AI stocks, including Oracle, Nvidia (NVDA), and CoreWeave (CRWV), continued to trend lower today.

Turning to economic releases, investors await a slate of Fed speeches that could provide key insights into the U.S. economy.

The U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.168%. WTI crude oil futures were trending higher, hovering near $58.05 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price increased to nearly $4,312 per ounce.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened higher today as record sessions for two major U.S. indexes boosted investor sentiment.

Asia-Pacific Markets Close Higher

Asia-Pacific markets closed higher following new records touched by the Dow Jones and the S&P 500, as well as the recent 25-basis-point interest rate cut by the U.S. Federal Reserve.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index closed 1.75% higher. In China, the Shanghai Composite rose 0.41%, while the Shenzhen Component was up 0.84%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei gained 1.37%, and the Topix rose 1.98%.

data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Uber Stock (UBER) Pops as Robotaxi Deal with WeRide (WRD) Goes Live in Dubai
data.description:
Uber Technologies (UBER), the global ride-hail giant, inched higher in pre-market trading today after it quietly switched on robotaxi rides in Dubai through a new partnership with WeRide (WRD). The launch gives Uber its first active autonomous pilot service in the Middle East.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Uber users in Dubai can now open the app and pick the 'Autonomous' option to book a WeRide robotaxi. The pilot goes live in Umm Suqeim and Jumeirah, two busy beachfront districts that draw steady visitor traffic. Tawasul, a local operator, will run the cars and support daily operations.

The two companies spent months testing the service after first announcing the plan in April. This early rollout lets them see how riders respond and how the vehicles perform during real trips in crowded areas.

Pilot Marks a Key Step Toward a Driverless Service

Each robotaxi still has a trained specialist on board to watch the system and guide riders when needed. This setup helps Dubai's transport regulator monitor safety data before clearing the next phase.

Uber and WeRide aim to move to a commercial driverless service in early 2026, depending on test results and regulatory approval.

If the pilot runs smoothly, Uber could strengthen its position in the growing autonomous mobility space, as the Dubai launch adds an important new market to its early-stage AV efforts.

Is UBER Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on UBER stock based on 26 Buys, three Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average UBER price target of $115.92 per share implies 35.67% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Looking for High-Yield Dividend Stocks? Citizens JMP Suggests 2 Names — One Offers a Massive 13% Yield
data.description:
As we head into 2026, it's only natural to want to set up the best possible portfolio for the new year. The key here is returns – we've seen bullish markets for several years now, and investors want to keep up that momentum. Dividend stocks can make a clear contribution.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Among the higher-yield corners of the market, business development companies (BDCs) stand out. These firms provide financing to small- and medium-sized businesses that often fall outside the traditional banking system, and like REITs, they benefit from favorable tax treatment when they distribute a large share of earnings to shareholders. That leads to high and usually reliable dividends.

Covering the BDC space for Citizens JMP, analyst Brian McKenna notes that despite recent underperformance, the fundamentals remain intact.

"Both the Alts and BDCs have underperformed over the past several months, although as we have written about at length, underlying fundamentals remain generally healthy for many (vs. perceptions across the marketplace today)," McKenna said. "We think the recent underperformance across the industry is largely unwarranted, specifically as the broader markets continue to trade at/near all-time highs, and we see (yet again) another compelling longer-term buying opportunity in these stocks."

For investors seeking out high-yield dividend payers, this is exactly what is needed: an attractive point of entry and strong dividend. McKenna has two names to offer, including one with a massive yield of 13%. Let's open up the TipRanks database and give them a closer look so we can find out what else they bring to the table.

Trinity Capital (TRIN)

The first company on our list, Trinity Capital, is an alternative asset manager that is structured for business purposes as an internally managed BDC. The company aims to provide its client firms with access to the credit market, and its investors with stable and consistent returns. Trinity invests its capital in a wide range of target firms, across several categories: tech, equipment, life sciences, sponsor finance, and asset-based lending. Since its founding in 2008, Trinity has poured some $5.1 billion into its investments. The company is based in Phoenix and operates in the US and Europe.

Trinity currently has $2.6 billion in assets under management, and boasts a market cap of $1.15 billion. The company makes careful vetting of its investment targets, keeping in mind its constant goal of maintaining a sound return for investors. This is usually returned via dividend distributions, and as of September this year Trinity has returned a cumulative $411 million through those payments.

The most recent dividend payment was declared on September 17 and paid out on October 15. The payment, of 51 cents per common share, annualizes to $2.04 and gives a forward yield of 13.5%. Trinity has been paying out dividends since 2021, has never missed a quarterly payment, and does have a history of adding special or supplemental dividends when appropriate.

In its last earnings report, covering 3Q25, Trinity reported a total investment income of $75.6 million. This was up more than 22% year-over-year and beat the forecast by almost $1.2 million. At the bottom line, Trinity reported a net investment income of $37 million, which translated to a per-share value of 52 cents. The company's net investment income was up 26% year-over-year, although the 52-cent per-share value missed expectations by a penny. We should note that the NII-per-share was sufficient to fully cover the last dividend.

For Citizens analyst McKenna, this company offers a combination of growth and a solid dividend. He writes, "Given several company-specific drivers at Trinity, the outlook for earnings growth and ROEs remains healthy. Specifically, we currently model 1% YOY growth in NII per share (and our 2026E NII totals $2.10 per share) and an 15.5% ROE in 2026, which should ultimately drive solid dividend coverage over the next 12+ months (we think TRIN's dividend coverage ratio troughed in 2025 at ~102%). Thus, we believe the current $0.51 per share quarterly dividend, or $2.04 per share annually (implying a 103% dividend coverage ratio), remains rock solid, equating to an attractive (and elevated) yield of 13.7% (as of writing), during a period when dividend coverage ratios for many in the group are set to decline notably, and in some cases, future dividends are potentially at risk depending on the magnitude of future base rates cuts (among other macro factors)…"

The 5-star analyst sums up with an optimistic outlook: "Given what we view to be a healthy outlook and accelerating momentum into 2026, we expect this strong outperformance will persist moving forward."

These comments back up McKenna's Outperform (i.e., Buy) rating on the shares, while his $17.50 price target points toward a 16% upside in the coming year. Add in the dividend yield, and this stock's one-year return can exceed 29%. (To watch McKenna's track record, click here)

Overall, Trinity has earned a Moderate Buy consensus rating from the Street, based on 6 recent reviews that include 5 Buys and 1 Sell. The stock is priced at $15.12 and its $16.50 average price target indicates a one-year gain of 9%. (See TRIN stock forecast)
data.author: Michael Marcus

data.title: Broadcom Stock (AVGO) Drops 6% — Why a Strong Q4 Still Spooked Investors
data.description:
Broadcom (AVGO) delivered another strong quarter, beating Wall Street's expectations on both revenue and earnings. Yet despite the solid results, AVGO stock slid more than 6% in pre-market trading on Friday. The drop stems largely from Broadcom's aggressive push into the AI chip market, which excites investors but also raises concerns about soaring costs and the unclear profitability of such massive long-term investments.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

For Q4 FY25, Broadcom posted earnings of $1.95 per share, beating analysts' expectations of $1.87. Meanwhile, revenue climbed 24% to a record $64 billion for the full fiscal year, driven largely by demand for AI semiconductors and continued momentum from VMware.

What Went Wrong with Broadcom Earnings Report?

Well, the answer is margin concerns. During the earnings call, Broadcom warned that profit margins will dip next quarter as AI-related revenue becomes a larger part of the business. To be precise, CFO Kirsten Spears stated that the first-quarter consolidated gross margin would be down about 100 basis points. The company also noted that margins throughout the year will depend heavily on the balance between its infrastructure, software, and semiconductor sales.

Interestingly, CEO Hock Tan highlighted a massive $73 billion order backlog for the next 18 months, but even that wasn't enough to ease worries.

Analysts Weigh in on Broadcom Margin Concerns

Top-rated analyst Kinngai Chan of Summit Insights added that margin worries aren't the only issue. Broadcom's deep dependence on just a few major AI customers is also weighing on investor confidence and contributed to the stock's decline.

Chan pointed out that the company's huge backlog still comes from just five customers, and many of the orders are for high-priced AI systems that carry lower margins. These system sales are expected to make up an even larger share of revenue in the second half of fiscal 2026, adding to investor concerns.

Meanwhile, five-star-rated analyst Gil Luria of D.A. Davidson noted that the drop in gross margins could signal a potential problem for Broadcom. He warned that rising costs tied to chip manufacturing partner TSMC (TSM) may limit how much profit Broadcom can generate from its custom AI processor business.

Is AVGO a Good Stock to Buy?

Currently, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Broadcom stock based on 24 Buys and one Hold assigned over the last three months. The average AVGO stock price target of $439.19 indicates 8.08% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Reddit Stock (RDDT) Falls despite Jefferies' Backing for 2026 amid AI Threats
data.description:
Shares in Reddit (RDDT), the California-based social news aggregator, fell about 2% on Thursday despite Jefferies' latest bullish view on the stock heading into 2026.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Jefferies analyst John Colantouni, in his "2026 Internet Playbook" note, reaffirmed his Buy rating on RDDT and elevated his price target by about 8% to $325 per share. This is even though Calantouni warned investors to be picky of internet stocks to add to their portfolio in 2026.

Analyst Flags Margin and AI Risks for 2026

According to the Jefferies analyst, such a cautious approach is key as he expects expanding investments by social media companies to continue to pressure the ability to grow profit margins. Colantouni also warned that AI disintermediation — AI tools sitting between users and traditional internet platforms — could curtail the level of multiple stock price appreciation in the incoming year.

Some investors have raised concerns about AI tools such as chatbots, search functions, and content aggregators, weakening traditional social media platforms by reducing traffic and ad impressions.

Reddit Remains 'Important' despite AI Threat

Colantouni's warning comes as data continues to show that Alphabet's (GOOGL) Gemini AI chatbot continued to attract massive mobile downloads following the mid-November release of Gemini 3.0, which has been described as Google's most powerful AI model yet.

According to official figures from Google, Gemini had 650 million monthly active users in October — up from 450 million in July. However, startup OpenAI (PC:OPAIQ) — which has issued a "code red" alert to defend its market share — continues to lead the race with 900 million weekly active users.

Meanwhile, Citizens analyst Andrew Boone previously contended that Reddit remains "increasingly important" to the open web ecosystem as search engines and chatbots rely on its content. Boone believes that Reddit's Answers feature would be a significant catalyst for the company in 2026.

Answers is Reddit's new default search function that uses AI to collate and summarize responses to users' queries.

Is Reddit a Strong Buy?

On Wall Street, Reddit's shares remain a Moderate Buy based on analyst consensus. This breaks down to 13 Buys and eight Holds issued over the three months.

At $255.25, the average RDDT price target implies more than 9% growth potential.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo
